• Wardley Maps on Vimeo - Talks by Simon Wardley and the Wardley mapping community.
• CGTFSstars4 - C library for reading static GTFS feeds. Supports reading unpacked feeds into application memory or into SQLite databases.
• RRRR Rapid Real-time Routingstars147 - RRRR (usually pronounced R4) is a C-language implementation of the RAPTOR public transit routing algorithm.

• gtfsdbstars124 - Python library for converting GTFS files into a relational database.
• gtfsmanstars20 - Repository-like tool in Python to manage and update a huge number of GTFS feeds.
• gtfspystars125 - Public transport network analysis and travel time computations using Python3. Compatible with Postgres/PostGIS, Oracle, MySQL, and SQLite. Used by gtfspy-webvizstars33.
• Make GTFS - A Python library to make GTFS feeds from basic route information.
• Mapzen GTFSstars27 - A Python GTFS library that supports reading individual GTFS tables, or constructing a graph to represent each agency in a feed.
• multigtfsstars49 - A Django application to import and export GTFS.
• GTFS-vizstars80 - Ruby script that converts a set of GTFS files into a SQLite database + GeoJSONs (needed by the Transit Mapstars325 web application)

• Lessons In Electric Circuits - Free high quality textbooks and worksheets with emphasis on theory, simulation, and the socratic method.
• Ultimate Electronics: Practical Circuit Design and Analysis - Free online book with interactive schematics & simulations by CircuitLab (under development).
• Khan Academy - Electrical Engineering - Non-profit learning platform with a full course on electrical engineering and related topics.
• ngspice - Open source SPICE circuit simulator.
• Circuit JS/Falstad - Free, open source online simulator with electron flow visualization (rewrite of original Java applet by Paul Falstad).
• EveryCircuit - Free to try online, visual, interactive circuit simulator for simpler circuits.
• DesignSpark PCB - Gratis EDA package without restrictions, sponserd by RS Components.
• Altium CircuitMaker - Free package from the maker of the go to pro software.
• Xesscorp's list of KiCad 3rd party toolsstars607
• KiCad.info Forums - User discussion and help forum.
• Footprint Collectionstars275 - Collection of all the KiCad footprints available online and some scripts to manage them.
• Hackster.io - Another social site for sharing projects. Is well organised by platform, topic and product.
• InventHub - Git-based project hosting and collaboration platform for hardware development.
• Hackster.io - Another blog covering electronics.
• /r/askelectronics - Sub-reddit dedicated to help on electronics topics.
• Electronics Stack Exchange - Question and answer site for electronics running on the popular Stack Overflow service.
• EEVBlog beginners forum - Good place for beginner questions, other sub-forums on EEVblog should be suitable for questions on more advanced topics.
• The Amp Hour - Off-the-cuff chat about electronics with Chris Gammel and Dave Jones (EEVBlog), often with guests
• MacroFab Engineering Podcast - Weekly podcast where Parker and Stephen from MacroFab discuss EE topics and industry news.
• The Engineering Commons Podcast - Covers general engineering topics from mechanical to electrical.
• GrabCad - Community supported database of 3D models with a large number of electronic component models.
• 3D ContentCentral - Website dedicated to 3D models of parts (requires login).
• PwnKitteh/InsanelyCheapElectronicsstars270 - A list of cheap electronics from China, that you can use in your projects.
• intajay/open-electronicsstars368 - Another GitHub list: resources for Electronics Enthusiasts and Hardware Hackers.
• Vitorian/awesome-fpgastars243 - Awesome list of FPGA resources.
• cajt/list_of_robot_electronicsstars231 - A GitHub list of resources, projects and products for robot electronics.
• embedded-boston/awesome-embedded-systemsstars579 - Awesome list of embedded programming resources.
• TCAD Central - List of Technology CAD (TCAD) software and resources from the maker of DEVSIM.
• deno-contextstars6 - Propagate deadlines, a cancellation and other request-scoped values to multiple promise. The behaviour is like Go's context.

Klask

Klask is a board game that has a ball field shape with two deep holes functioning as goals in each end of the field. The purpose of the game is to push the small ball around on the field with your magnet/gaming piece, shoot the ball past the obstacles and your opponent and into the goal hole.

Players Min. Age Time
2 8 10m
